audits.excerpt: In the silence Sylvanas could picture her anyway: back to the wall, wrists chained, braid half ruined by rough palace hands, one side of her face still bruised where Frostmourne's guard had caught her in the chamber.
audits.reason: The prose slips out of cell acoustics into a free-camera reconstruction. That undercuts the confinement pressure the scene has been building through breath, chain, and masonry.
audits.suggestedFix: In the silence chain rasped once, then breath came again from higher in the cell, careful and upright against the wall.

audits.excerpt: A raised bench built for dynastic confidence rather than doubt. It might once have been used for petitions, censures, disputes over land and oath.
audits.reason: The room drifts into institutional summary while Sylvanas is still being physically placed under guard. The hall needs present-tense pressure, not speculative courtroom history.
audits.suggestedFix: A raised bench loomed over the flagstones with three dark chairs, stacked papers, and enough polished wood to make every word sound official.

audits.reason: This lands as a conceptual label instead of a visible condition. After the strong chain detail, the sentence pair briefly turns schematic.
audits.suggestedFix: Not dungeon iron but courtroom iron: light enough to walk in, loose enough for every bench in the hall to read prisoner before person.

audits.excerpt:
She had built it out of truths.

A trap built to snap at the one point appearances were already weakest.
audits.reason: The prosecution's pressure lifts off page into summary metaphor. The scene needs paper, desk, gallery, and sequence pressure, not authorial compression.
audits.suggestedFix: Every true fact Ashcombe laid down left another marked page on Thornton's desk and another head in the gallery turning her way before the next lie bent it.

audits.excerpt: Then the lid came up farther, and everyone in the room felt the edge of the wrongness inside it.
audits.reason: The collective reaction is rendered as generic shared sensation. Frostmourne works better when the response is distributed through specific bodies in the chamber.
audits.suggestedFix: Then the lid came up farther. The front bench recoiled first, one priest caught the rail, and breath broke across the room in one ragged pull.

audits.excerpt: The whole room felt the cruelty in it because she did not raise her voice.
audits.reason: This tells the emotional effect in one abstract sweep when the cross-examination already has better carriers in benches, pens, and stillness.
audits.suggestedFix: No one on the benches moved. Even the clerk's pen paused before it scratched on again.

audits.excerpt: She held that in place at once. Not hope. Shape. A line that might still touch the outside of this prison.
audits.reason: The interior turn goes schematic just when the scene needs a usable external fact. The paragraph should stay with corroboration, not emotional math.
audits.suggestedFix: She held one fact in place: elven mages above the vault had felt the same wrongness through the paving.

audits.reason: The judgment stack is too abstract and self-contained. It flattens Lor'themar's report into pure valuation instead of letting the source fact carry the dread.
audits.suggestedFix: Good that the mages had felt it. Horrible that the feeling had already reached the paving outside the lower court.
